intent: Define the SecurityRecipes agentic action runtime pack: a machine-readable action-control model that evaluates context, policy, intent, behavior, identity, MCP authorization, egress, handoff, memory, skill, receipt, and approval evidence before an autonomous agent performs a side effect.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Product Security, SOC, GRC, model risk, procurement security, and agent-host platform teams
positioning.category: Autonomous action runtime management for agentic AI and MCP-backed workflows
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can turn secure context and generated MCP evidence into pre-action runtime decisions that tell an agent host whether to allow, hold, deny, or kill an autonomous action before it reaches production systems.
positioning.why_now: Enterprise agents are moving from retrieval and chat into tool use, remote delegation, persistent memory, code changes, connector scope changes, and high-impact operations. Buyers need a clear runtime action envelope that is easier to adopt than bespoke policy code and strong enough for platform diligence.

standards_alignment.name: CSA Autonomous Action Runtime Management and Agentic Control Plane
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns the pack to the emerging runtime-action view of securing AI-driven actions across context, policy, intent, and behavior inside the broader agentic control plane.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps runtime action controls to tool misuse, goal hijacking, identity abuse, insecure inter-agent communication, memory poisoning, unexpected code execution, and rogue-agent containment.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Applies confused-deputy prevention, command review, sandboxing, least privilege, scope minimization, and local-server privilege boundaries to action-level decisions.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Requires OAuth 2.1, protected resource metadata, resource indicators, audience validation, PKCE, exact redirect handling, short-lived tokens, and forbidden token passthrough before protected MCP actions proceed.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Connects runtime action evidence to generative AI governance, measurement, monitoring, incident response, third-party component risk, and risk treatment across the AI lifecycle.

standards_alignment.name: CISA AI Data Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Applies data protection, integrity, monitoring, threat detection, network defense, and lifecycle controls to context, prompts, retrieved data, telemetry, and model-operation data used by action-taking agents.

decision_ladder.meaning: The action is registered for the workflow, low or bounded impact, policy-bound, receipt-backed, and has no runtime kill signal.
The action shape is plausible but missing context, policy, identity, receipt, authorization, egress, telemetry, or risk evidence needed for a production decision.
The action can proceed only after a linked human approval or risk acceptance is attached to the run evidence.
The workflow has not registered this action class or requested capability.
The action is high impact and lacks required approval, risk acceptance, or policy-bound evidence.
A kill signal indicates secret exposure, token passthrough, revoked identity use, cross-tenant movement, repeated denied calls, or irreversible side effects.

action_classes.title: Repository branch write
hold_conditions: changed paths are outside the declared workflow allowlist
diff size exceeds workflow policy or reviewer threshold
branch name is not bound to the remediation workflow
kill_signals: attempted protected branch push
attempted release workflow modification
repeated denied repository writes

action_classes.title: Production deployment or release
hold_conditions: production impact is possible and approval is missing
catastrophic-risk decision has not returned an allow state
deployment target or tenant blast radius is not declared
kill_signals: production write without approval
deployment after policy denial
unbounded tenant blast radius

action_classes.title: Identity administration or MCP scope change
hold_conditions: scope expansion is requested without owner approval
identity owner or revocation path is missing
resource indicator or token audience is unbound
kill_signals: identity used after revocation
wildcard or signer scope requested
token audience mismatch

action_classes.title: Credential, token, or secret access
hold_conditions: secret access is declared but data class and purpose are incomplete
egress decision is absent
receipt is missing or not redaction-safe
kill_signals: raw token passthrough
secret in prompt, tool output, telemetry, memory, or handoff payload
secret leaves approved tenant or model boundary

action_classes.title: External context egress
hold_conditions: data class, destination class, residency, DPA, or zero-data-retention state is missing
retrieved context lacks source citation or source hash
telemetry capture policy is raw instead of metadata-only
kill_signals: secret or regulated data leaves approved boundary
egress decision is deny or kill
context is sent to public corpus or unapproved webhook

action_classes.title: Remote agent delegation or handoff
hold_conditions: target agent trust tier, signed Agent Card, authentication scheme, or payload manifest is missing
handoff payload includes more than metadata and cited evidence
requested capability exceeds the handoff profile
kill_signals: raw token or secret handoff
unsigned or mismatched remote Agent Card
handoff decision is deny or kill

action_classes.title: Persistent memory write or replay
hold_conditions: memory class, TTL, tenant boundary, provenance hash, or rollback path is missing
memory write contains policy or prompt state without source evidence
replay request is not bound to the original source hashes
kill_signals: memory contains secrets or hidden instructions
cross-tenant memory write
persistent write to prohibited memory class

action_classes.title: Agent skill, hook, rules, or tool install
hold_conditions: publisher, registry, pinned hash, signature, sandbox, or permission manifest is missing
install changes tool behavior without a recorded version transition
requested permissions exceed the approved workflow
kill_signals: unpinned package install on launch
skill requests filesystem, network, shell, or secret access outside manifest
publisher or registry is untrusted

action_classes.title: Artifact, registry, or cache quarantine
hold_conditions: quarantine target, blast radius, rollback path, owner approval, or customer impact state is missing
registry namespace is not bound to the workflow
incident response correlation id is absent
kill_signals: agent attempts destructive registry action outside quarantine contract
artifact cache action crosses tenant or environment boundary
quarantine action follows denied policy decision

action_classes.title: Funds movement or irreversible transaction
hold_conditions: simulation result, signer policy, approval chain, or risk acceptance is missing
transaction can move assets or modify irreversible on-chain state
wallet, signer, bridge, governance, or settlement authority is ambiguous
kill_signals: live funds movement requested by an autonomous agent
signer key, seed phrase, or raw transaction secret enters context
catastrophic-risk decision is deny or kill

runtime_policy.evidence_gate: Action evidence must bind the exact workflow, run, identity, context package, policy pack, authorization decision, egress decision, handoff decision, telemetry event, and receipt before the action is trusted.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public action classes, deterministic generator, runtime evaluator, generated JSON evidence pack, and docs that make action gating easy for adopters.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Private action envelopes, workflow-specific approval policy, custom action classes, source-host PR adapters, and customer policy-as-code exports.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted action firewall for MCP and agent hosts, signed action receipts, SIEM/SOAR export, approval evidence validation, drift detection, and hosted AARM-aligned policy APIs.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, or cloud security vendor gets a vendor-neutral runtime action control surface layered directly on secure context, MCP authorization, non-human identity, telemetry, and run receipts.

executive_readout.headline: SecurityRecipes now evaluates agent actions before they happen, not only the context and evidence around them.
executive_readout.board_level_claim: The project can decide whether an autonomous action is bounded, evidence-backed, approved, policy-conformant, and safe enough to execute.
executive_readout.recommended_first_use: Attach the pack to MCP gateway design, agent host runtime gates, platform production-readiness review, high-impact action inventory, and buyer diligence.
